LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SANDRA YANG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES, FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PLEAD THAT THEY SUFFERED LOST MONEY OR PROPERTY AS A RESULT OF THE UNFAIR COMPETITION. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT NOT A MATTER THAT MAY BE JUDICIALLY NOTICED WHERE THE PARTIES DISPUTE THE INTERPRETATION OF THE AGREEMENT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) (302/CWW)JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR# 8620

APPEARANCES: Jethro S. Busch, Esq. of Steven Adair MacDonald & Associates, P.C. 956-6488 for pltfs & opposing parties; Lisa M. Garcia, Esq. of Bledsoe, Cathcart, Pedersen & Treppa LLP 951-2825 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission as to defendant's request for sanctions only and adopted its tentative ruling as to the remainder - Defendant's motion to compel further discovery responses is denied as to Form Interrogatories 2.12, 2.13 and 12.1. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SANDRA YANG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st cause of action, unfair business practices, for plaintiffs to plead that they suffered lost money or property as a result of the unfair competition. Demurrer overruled as to all other causes of action. Settlement agreement not a matter that may be judicially noticed where the parties dispute the interpretation of the agreement. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR# 8620

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SANDRA YANG MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted with 10 days leave to amend as to claim for punitive damages only, for plaintiffs to plead malice, fraud, or oppression. Motion denied as moot as to the 2nd, 4th and 5th causes of action. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR# 8620
